title: Ensure 'Minimum Inbound TLS Version' is set to '1.2' or  higher

input_row.title: Ensure 'Minimum Inbound TLS Version' is set to '1.2' or  higher
input_row.description: The TLS (Transport Layer Security) protocol secures the transmission of data over the internet using standard encryption technology. Function app deployment slots use TLS 1.2 for the Minimum Inbound TLS Version by default and allow for the use of TLS versions 1.0, 1.1, and 1.3. NIST strongly suggests the use of TLS 1.2 and recommends the adoption of TLS 1.3.
input_row.rationale: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are outdated and vulnerable to security risks. Since TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 provide enhanced security and improved performance, it is highly recommended to use TLS 1.2 or higher whenever possible.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, ensure that Minimum Inbound TLS Version is set to 1.2 or higher. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each function app and deployment slot. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list function apps: az functionapp list For each function app, run the following command to list deployment slots: az functionapp deployment slot list --resource-group -- name For each deployment slot, run the following command to get the TLS version setting: az resource show --name web --resource-group -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites//slots/ --query properties.minTlsVersion Ensure that "1.2" or higher is returned. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: deb528de-8f89-4101-881c-595899253102 - Name: 'Function app slots should use the latest TLS version'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, click the drop-down next to Minimum Inbound TLS Version and select 1.2 or higher. 7. Click Save. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each function app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to update the TLS version: az resource update --name web --resource-group -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites//slots/ --set properties.minTlsVersion=<1.2|1.3>
input_row.impact: Using the latest TLS version may affect compatibility with clients and backend services.
input_row.default_value: By default, TLS version is set to 1.2.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_lambda_function_minimum_tls_version_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. Use the AWS CLI or boto3 SDK to query the properties of AWS Lambda functions, particularly their configuration settings related to TLS.
2. The specific resource to enumerate includes AWS Lambda functions and their configurations.
3. The AWS CLI command to list Lambda functions is: `aws lambda list-functions`.
4. For each function, use the configuration call: `aws lambda get-function-configuration --function-name <function_name>`.
5. Validate the `MinimumTlsVersion` setting (this will require checking AWS documentation or testing if this property exists as AWS may not expose it directly but confirm through the function's configuration).
6. Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
   - Check if the value of the `MinimumTlsVersion` property is set to "1.2" or higher.
   - If it is "1.2" or "1.3", return pass.
   - If it is "1.0" or "1.1", return fail.
   - If the property is not set, assume it defaults to "1.2" and return pass based on this assumption as per AWS documentation.

**Rationale for Automated Approach:**
- The control focuses on configuring security settings related to TLS for AWS Lambda functions, which is a standard AWS property that can be audited programmatically via AWS APIs or through CLI commands.
- This control is applicable in the context of ensuring the secure transmission of data, and AWS supports querying relevant configurations directly, allowing full automation of this audit.
